name: ai-patient-context-card
title: AI Patient Context Card
description: Expanded patient/encounter scope for an assistant request, with identity, alerts, and last-updated slots.

props.name: name
props.description: Required. Display name — you decide what is safe to show on this surface.

props.name: initials
props.description: Avatar initials. Falls back to a generic person icon.

props.name: identifiers
props.description: Identifiers you have chosen to display (age/sex, MRN, DOB). Passed as a node, never assembled here.

props.name: lastUpdated
props.description: When the underlying context was last refreshed.

clinicalNotes: Consumers decide which identifiers are safe to render here, which is why there is no `patient` object prop and no default demographic payload — a component that happily accepts a full record will end up displaying one on a shared screen, in a screenshot, or in a support ticket. Pass only what the surface warrants. `lastUpdated` matters more than it looks: an assistant answering from context pulled twenty minutes ago, before the last set of observations, is answering about a patient who no longer exists, and the clinician needs to see that staleness rather than infer it. The alert badges are presentational — this component does not check allergies, it displays checks your product already ran.
notes: The expanded counterpart to an ai-context-chip of kind="patient"; use it where the scope deserves a full card (a workspace header) rather than a token.
